H1项目奖.project_name: SEA E-Commerce Ads TR Improvement Project
H1项目奖.reason:
The e-Commerce and monetisation teams focused on separate goals—GMV growth vs. revenue and Take Rate (TR). To unlock sustainable growth, the SEA E-Commerce Ads TR Improvement Project aligned both objectives into a self-reinforcing cycle: grow revenue, reinvest in users and merchants, boost GMV, and repeat.
This was achieved through two major efforts: embedding ad value into the organic distribution system and fostering close cross-team collaboration. By introducing eCPM-based interventions across all traffic endpoints—from search to feeds—the project optimized recommendation logic for both user experience and platform profit. As a result, ad TR rose from 5.5% to 7.3%, while GMAX’s GMV share jumped from 32% to 49%, and income share from 70% to 88%—the fastest TR growth seen across all e-Commerce platforms.
Importantly, this was done without compromising GMV. In reverse experiments, ads ADVV increased by 14%, proving value-added traffic efficiency.
The project also laid the groundwork for long-term system unification: organic and ads systems are now converging with shared value signals, cutting redundant development and data costs.
A “shared-metrics” culture underpins this success. The organic team now owns profitability as a KPI, while the ads team incorporates GMV and user experience into its goals. Advertising has evolved into a core business lever—integrated into merchant tools, creator incentives, payment systems, and even AM performance evaluations. OKRs across functions are fully aligned.
This project integrated monetisation and growth into one unified engine driving platform success.

This project integrated monetisation and growth into one unified engine driving platform success.

H1项目奖.project_name: SEA E-Commerce Ads TR Improvement Project
H1项目奖.reason:
The e-Commerce and monetisation teams focused on separate goals—GMV growth vs. revenue and Take Rate (TR). To unlock sustainable growth, the SEA E-Commerce Ads TR Improvement Project aligned both objectives into a self-reinforcing cycle: grow revenue, reinvest in users and merchants, boost GMV, and repeat.
This was achieved through two major efforts: embedding ad value into the organic distribution system and fostering close cross-team collaboration. By introducing eCPM-based interventions across all traffic endpoints—from search to feeds—the project optimized recommendation logic for both user experience and platform profit. As a result, ad TR rose from 5.5% to 7.3%, while GMAX’s GMV share jumped from 32% to 49%, and income share from 70% to 88%—the fastest TR growth seen across all e-Commerce platforms.
Importantly, this was done without compromising GMV. In reverse experiments, ads ADVV increased by 14%, proving value-added traffic efficiency.

A “shared-metrics” culture underpins this success. The organic team now owns profitability as a KPI, while the ads team incorporates GMV and user experience into its goals. Advertising has evolved into a core business lever—integrated into merchant tools, creator incentives, payment systems, and even AM performance evaluations. OKRs across functions are fully aligned.
This project integrated monetisation and growth into one unified engine driving platform success.

The project also laid the groundwork for long-term system unification: organic and ads systems are now converging with shared value signals, cutting redundant development and data costs.
A “shared-metrics” culture underpins this success. The organic team now owns profitability as a KPI, while the ads team incorporates GMV and user experience into its goals. Advertising has evolved into a core business lever—integrated into merchant tools, creator incentives, payment systems, and even AM performance evaluations. OKRs across functions are fully aligned.
This project integrated monetisation and growth into one unified engine driving platform success.

H1项目奖.project_name: US FBT (Fulfillment by TikTok) Cost Efficiency Project
H1项目奖.reason:
**Cost**: FC+LM cost optimization meets expectations, Q2 (6.1-6.21) FC +LM costs $xx/order (vs. June target $xx/order), a reduction of $xx/order compared with Q1 (March); mainly due to
- **[Vendor Choice] Radically reduced the business proportion of A provider , from 4.0% in March to 0.2% in June (contributing - $xx/order).** Ops suspended A's inventory from Nov 2024, and started inventory transfers from April 2025. By the last week of June, 1.6M units transferred out of A. Meanwhile, product side supported lowering A priority and ran simulations to facilitate the process.
- **[Vendor Choice] Introduce ** **more price-competitive service providers, with coverage of low-price regional service providers increasing from 36% in March to 55% in June (contributing -$xx/order)**. In Q2, a total of 12 3DD last-mile service providers were in use (14 types of services in total): integrated 3 ultra-low-price services and shortened the integration cycle to 21 days, with coverage reaching 17% by the end of June; promoted further price reduction of B provider and significantly increased postal code coverage, rising from 18% in March to 34% in June; further optimized the product side allocation strategy of National carriers, advanced FBT direct-connected C service provider to replace the more expensive D service provider , with C coverage increasing from 14% in March to 19% in June.
- **[Product optimization] Launch order consolidation engine to increase order consolidation rate and decrease split rate(-$xx/order)**:we adjust the consolidation logic to increase the consolidation order pool. In addition, by replacing the packing algorithm, we increase the packing utilization rate for consolidated packages. Additionally, through broader prediction coverage and improved accuracy for frequently bought-together SKUs, the multi-order split rate dropped from 66% to 22%, and the consolidation rate has increased from 4% to 12%.
- **[Operation & Product Optimization] **Optimized packaging consumables recommendation and box usage management, reducing box% from 66% to 41%, and promoted direct-procured packaging to be applied in JD warehouse**. The actual cost contribution of packaging consumables used is -$xx/order. **Continuously improve the on-site processes of the West Coast CostPlus model warehouse and enhance efficiency, strengthen labor hour management to reduce costs,  IB+OB total cost reduction -$xx/item。
- **[Product optimazation]Optimize Inventory Placement (-$xx/order)**:Through the allocation strategy optimization of Inventory Planning Engine, the improvement of demand forecasting and the optimization of warehouse distribution routes, the optimal placement improved from 69% in Q1 to 76% in Q2 QTD, driving a reduction of longzone shipments from 21.6% in Q1 to 17.6% Q2 QTD.

**Cost**: FC+LM cost optimization meets expectations, Q2 (6.1-6.21) FC +LM costs $xx/order (vs. June target $xx/order), a reduction of $xx/order compared with Q1 (March); mainly due to
- **[Vendor Choice] Radically reduced the business proportion of A provider , from 4.0% in March to 0.2% in June (contributing - $xx/order).** Ops suspended A's inventory from Nov 2024, and started inventory transfers from April 2025. By the last week of June, 1.6M units transferred out of A. Meanwhile, product side supported lowering A priority and ran simulations to facilitate the process.
- **[Vendor Choice] Introduce ** **more price-competitive service providers, with coverage of low-price regional service providers increasing from 36% in March to 55% in June (contributing -$xx/order)**. In Q2, a total of 12 3DD last-mile service providers were in use (14 types of services in total): integrated 3 ultra-low-price services and shortened the integration cycle to 21 days, with coverage reaching 17% by the end of June; promoted further price reduction of B provider and significantly increased postal code coverage, rising from 18% in March to 34% in June; further optimized the product side allocation strategy of National carriers, advanced FBT direct-connected C service provider to replace the more expensive D service provider , with C coverage increasing from 14% in March to 19% in June.
- **[Product optimization] Launch order consolidation engine to increase order consolidation rate and decrease split rate(-$xx/order)**:we adjust the consolidation logic to increase the consolidation order pool. In addition, by replacing the packing algorithm, we increase the packing utilization rate for consolidated packages. Additionally, through broader prediction coverage and improved accuracy for frequently bought-together SKUs, the multi-order split rate dropped from 66% to 22%, and the consolidation rate has increased from 4% to 12%.
- **[Operation & Product Optimization] **Optimized packaging consumables recommendation and box usage management, reducing box% from 66% to 41%, and promoted direct-procured packaging to be applied in JD warehouse**. The actual cost contribution of packaging consumables used is -$xx/order. **Continuously improve the on-site processes of the West Coast CostPlus model warehouse and enhance efficiency, strengthen labor hour management to reduce costs,  IB+OB total cost reduction -$xx/item。
- **[Product optimazation]Optimize Inventory Placement (-$xx/order)**:Through the allocation strategy optimization of Inventory Planning Engine, the improvement of demand forecasting and the optimization of warehouse distribution routes, the optimal placement improved from 69% in Q1 to 76% in Q2 QTD, driving a reduction of longzone shipments from 21.6% in Q1 to 17.6% Q2 QTD.

Established standardized regulatory inquiry mechanisms with UK OPSS, France DGCCRF, and Germany’s Market Surveillance Authority, covering the full process from inquiry intake and internal handling to response review.
Built internal and external (LegitScript) product compliance cleanliness scoring systems to monitor unsafe recalled products and assess control effectiveness in real time, achieving a 95%+ recall cleanliness rate on the internal platform.

H2项目奖.department: 产品研发和工程架构-Global E-Commerce

H2项目奖.reason:
[Project Impact]
In 2025, the project successfully moved Brazil’s live commerce business from exploration to scaled growth, delivering systematic breakthroughs across user quality, content supply, and ROI efficiency. Live GMV contribution increased from 8% mid-year to 25% by year-end, reaching 28% in January 2026, with daily GMV stabilizing at the million-dollar level—making it one of the most representative growth benchmarks in the region.
[Project Highlights]
User Quality Upgrade
Live commerce cultivated a cohort of high-quality consumers with stronger purchase intent and more stable repeat behavior. Live users achieved an RPD30 of 0.988—1.39× and 1.82× that of short video and product card formats respectively — and overall AOV outperformed the market by 10%. As live supply and scenarios continued to expand, user consumption habits within live sessions strengthened, creating a sustainable and repeatable growth engine.
Content & Supply Structure Breakthrough
The influencer-driven live model scaled significantly, contributing over 80% of total live GMV. Through creator incentives, agency policies, ranking campaigns, and merchant live rebates, daily live sessions surpassed 10,000. The project cultivated local KOLs reaching multi-million GMV scale, with celebrities and top beauty brands surpassing $50K per session. Leading local brands such as Natura, along with GKA brands including B.O. and Avène, actively invested in merchant-led live streaming. These large-scale, high-impact live events not only drove GMV breakthroughs but also significantly elevated brand and user awareness of live commerce.
Operational Efficiency Optimization
Starting in Q4 2025, the project shifted focus from “expanding supply” to “improving quality.” By optimizing subsidy allocation, tightening entry and exit thresholds, and refining incentive mechanisms, resources were redirected toward high-conversion, high-repeat live scenarios. While sustaining growth momentum, ROI improved significantly, with live subsidy rates reduced from 2.8% to 1.6%, achieving simultaneous scale expansion and efficiency gains.

The influencer-driven live model scaled significantly, contributing over 80% of total live GMV. Through creator incentives, agency policies, ranking campaigns, and merchant live rebates, daily live sessions surpassed 10,000. The project cultivated local KOLs reaching multi-million GMV scale, with celebrities and top beauty brands surpassing $50K per session. Leading local brands such as Natura, along with GKA brands including B.O. and Avène, actively invested in merchant-led live streaming. These large-scale, high-impact live events not only drove GMV breakthroughs but also significantly elevated brand and user awareness of live commerce.
Operational Efficiency Optimization
Starting in Q4 2025, the project shifted focus from “expanding supply” to “improving quality.” By optimizing subsidy allocation, tightening entry and exit thresholds, and refining incentive mechanisms, resources were redirected toward high-conversion, high-repeat live scenarios. While sustaining growth momentum, ROI improved significantly, with live subsidy rates reduced from 2.8% to 1.6%, achieving simultaneous scale expansion and efficiency gains.

[Project Impact]
In 2025, the project successfully moved Brazil’s live commerce business from exploration to scaled growth, delivering systematic breakthroughs across user quality, content supply, and ROI efficiency. Live GMV contribution increased from 8% mid-year to 25% by year-end, reaching 28% in January 2026, with daily GMV stabilizing at the million-dollar level—making it one of the most representative growth benchmarks in the region.
[Project Highlights]
User Quality Upgrade
Live commerce cultivated a cohort of high-quality consumers with stronger purchase intent and more stable repeat behavior. Live users achieved an RPD30 of 0.988—1.39× and 1.82× that of short video and product card formats respectively — and overall AOV outperformed the market by 10%. As live supply and scenarios continued to expand, user consumption habits within live sessions strengthened, creating a sustainable and repeatable growth engine.

These large-scale, high-impact live events not only drove GMV breakthroughs but also significantly elevated brand and user awareness of live commerce.
Operational Efficiency Optimization
Starting in Q4 2025, the project shifted focus from “expanding supply” to “improving quality.” By optimizing subsidy allocation, tightening entry and exit thresholds, and refining incentive mechanisms, resources were redirected toward high-conversion, high-repeat live scenarios. While sustaining growth momentum, ROI improved significantly, with live subsidy rates reduced from 2.8% to 1.6%, achieving simultaneous scale expansion and efficiency gains.

[Project Impact]
In 2025, the project successfully moved Brazil’s live commerce business from exploration to scaled growth, delivering systematic breakthroughs across user quality, content supply, and ROI efficiency. Live GMV contribution increased from 8% mid-year to 25% by year-end, reaching 28% in January 2026, with daily GMV stabilizing at the million-dollar level—making it one of the most representative growth benchmarks in the region.
[Project Highlights]
User Quality Upgrade
Live commerce cultivated a cohort of high-quality consumers with stronger purchase intent and more stable repeat behavior. Live users achieved an RPD30 of 0.988—1.39× and 1.82× that of short video and product card formats respectively — and overall AOV outperformed the market by 10%. As live supply and scenarios continued to expand, user consumption habits within live sessions strengthened, creating a sustainable and repeatable growth engine.
Content & Supply Structure Breakthrough
The influencer-driven live model scaled significantly, contributing over 80% of total live GMV. Through creator incentives, agency policies, ranking campaigns, and merchant live rebates, daily live sessions surpassed 10,000. The project cultivated local KOLs reaching multi-million GMV scale, with celebrities and top beauty brands surpassing $50K per session. Leading local brands such as Natura, along with GKA brands including B.O. and Avène, actively invested in merchant-led live streaming. These large-scale, high-impact live events not only drove GMV breakthroughs but also significantly elevated brand and user awareness of live commerce.
Operational Efficiency Optimization
Starting in Q4 2025, the project shifted focus from “expanding supply” to “improving quality.” By optimizing subsidy allocation, tightening entry and exit thresholds, and refining incentive mechanisms, resources were redirected toward high-conversion, high-repeat live scenarios. While sustaining growth momentum, ROI improved significantly, with live subsidy rates reduced from 2.8% to 1.6%, achieving simultaneous scale expansion and efficiency gains.

H2项目奖.project_name: 美国 GMV Max 全切与大促绑定
H2项目奖.reason:
In the early stages, U.S. merchants and partners had concerns about GMV Max, stemming from both product gaps and misunderstandings.
Following in-depth discussions with Mason in Zhuhai in June, the team conducted focused GMV Max Q&A sessions, multiple rounds of communication, and joint research to distinguish product gaps from [misunderstandings.By](http://misunderstandings.By) September, GMV Max revenue penetration reached 95%, allowing us to successfully phase out ROI1. This transparent cross-functional collaboration built the trust needed for our next breakthrough.
In Q4, with a "Deliver the Mission" mindset, we achieved a global first by bundling GMV Max with US BFCM. This drove GMV Max GMV penetration from 65% to 81%, and the non-FS takerate reached 18% during peak campaign. These results validated our product’s value and successfully met the ambitious goal of Margin 2 breakeven in the U.S. market.
H2项目奖.project_english_name: US GMV Max Full Migration & Campaign Integration

H2项目奖.project_name: US POP 科技单治理与官方物流切换
H2项目奖.reason: [Background] Some US POP merchants used unpaid postage labels (UPO) to avoid shipping costs and gain unfair price advantages. This led to unhealthy competition and serious user experience issues once USPS stopped deliveries and charged customers. The platform needed decisive governance to restore fairness and protect user trust.[Challenges]Governance could impact short-term GMV but inaction would harm user experience.UPO detection relied on delayed and limited USPS signals, making suspected cases harder to handle.Policy rollout timing conflicted with holiday and Black Friday preparations.[Strategy] A three-phase, tiered rollout ultimately restricted 3PL usage across merchants:Phase 1: Full 3PL ban for confirmed UPO violators.Phase 2: Restrictions of seller shipping with USPS for high-risk merchants.Phase 3: POP-wide 3PL restriction (except those not accepted by TT shipping)[Impact]UPO signals reduced from 3,900 weekly orders to zero.Postage-related cancellation rate dropped from 22.34% to 4.63%.190K merchants transitioned from 3PL in Q4.Official logistics penetration rose from 13.5% to 92.1% (+78.6pp).Ultra-low-price competition declined significantly.The initiative restored fair competition, protected user trust during Black Friday, and accelerated official logistics scale and capability upgrades.[Reflection] Early warning signals appeared in July, but action was delayed due to GMV concerns. Going forward, risks must be identified and addressed earlier to better balance growth and user experience.
H2项目奖.project_english_name: US POP Tech Order Governance & Official Logistics Migration

The platform needed decisive governance to restore fairness and protect user trust.[Challenges]Governance could impact short-term GMV but inaction would harm user experience.UPO detection relied on delayed and limited USPS signals, making suspected cases harder to handle.Policy rollout timing conflicted with holiday and Black Friday preparations.[Strategy] A three-phase, tiered rollout ultimately restricted 3PL usage across merchants:Phase 1: Full 3PL ban for confirmed UPO violators.Phase 2: Restrictions of seller shipping with USPS for high-risk merchants.Phase 3: POP-wide 3PL restriction (except those not accepted by TT shipping)[Impact]UPO signals reduced from 3,900 weekly orders to zero.Postage-related cancellation rate dropped from 22.34% to 4.63%.190K merchants transitioned from 3PL in Q4.Official logistics penetration rose from 13.5% to 92.1% (+78.6pp).Ultra-low-price competition declined significantly.The initiative restored fair competition, protected user trust during Black Friday, and accelerated official logistics scale and capability upgrades.[Reflection] Early warning signals appeared in July, but action was delayed due to GMV concerns. Going forward, risks must be identified and addressed earlier to better balance growth and user experience.
H2项目奖.department: 供应链与物流-商家物流履约产品
H2项目奖.project_english_name: US POP Tech Order Governance & Official Logistics Migration

H2项目奖.project_name: SEA 机器与物流成本优化专项
H2项目奖.reason: The core optimization focus of algorithms and services has been GMV growth, while the SEA business simultaneously places strong emphasis on profitability. Cost savings can be converted 100% into incremental profit. In 2025, recommendation, search, logistics algorithms, architecture, backend, DS, and operations teams worked closely together to tackle two key cost drivers: machine cost and logistics cost. The initiative delivered annualized cost savings of $119.57M, becoming a major profit engine beyond core GMV growth.Cross-functional collaboration broke down the business–tech barriers to drive cost optimization:On machine resource optimization, the algorithm team analyzed traffic value and feature importance, while the architecture team implemented caching and compute scheduling improvements. Together they optimized inference allocation, removed low-contribution features, and eliminated redundant services and data, saving $35.15M annually.On the backend side, architecture improved core component utilization (TCE 30% → 42%), completed public cloud migration, and enhanced peak elasticity, reducing rates by 35% and saving $45.00M annually.On logistics cost, traffic algorithms integrated Zone and same-island factors into recommendation strategies, reducing logistics costs in ID/VN by 8.41% and saving $12.52M annually.Logistics dispatch algorithms were upgraded and extended to instant and same-day scenarios, delivering $26.90M in annual savings.This cost optimization initiative aligned with SEA’s transition from scale-driven growth to high-quality growth, striking a sustainable balance between expansion and profitability while establishing a reusable methodology and cross-team collaboration model for future efficiency improvements.
H2项目奖.project_english_name: SEA Machine & Logistics Cost Optimization Initiative

The initiative delivered annualized cost savings of $119.57M, becoming a major profit engine beyond core GMV growth.Cross-functional collaboration broke down the business–tech barriers to drive cost optimization:On machine resource optimization, the algorithm team analyzed traffic value and feature importance, while the architecture team implemented caching and compute scheduling improvements. Together they optimized inference allocation, removed low-contribution features, and eliminated redundant services and data, saving $35.15M annually.On the backend side, architecture improved core component utilization (TCE 30% → 42%), completed public cloud migration, and enhanced peak elasticity, reducing rates by 35% and saving $45.00M annually.On logistics cost, traffic algorithms integrated Zone and same-island factors into recommendation strategies, reducing logistics costs in ID/VN by 8.41% and saving $12.52M annually.Logistics dispatch algorithms were upgraded and extended to instant and same-day scenarios, delivering $26.90M in annual savings.This cost optimization initiative aligned with SEA’s transition from scale-driven growth to high-quality growth, striking a sustainable balance between expansion and profitability while establishing a reusable methodology and cross-team collaboration model for future efficiency improvements.

In 2025, recommendation, search, logistics algorithms, architecture, backend, DS, and operations teams worked closely together to tackle two key cost drivers: machine cost and logistics cost. The initiative delivered annualized cost savings of $119.57M, becoming a major profit engine beyond core GMV growth.Cross-functional collaboration broke down the business–tech barriers to drive cost optimization:On machine resource optimization, the algorithm team analyzed traffic value and feature importance, while the architecture team implemented caching and compute scheduling improvements. Together they optimized inference allocation, removed low-contribution features, and eliminated redundant services and data, saving $35.15M annually.On the backend side, architecture improved core component utilization (TCE 30% → 42%), completed public cloud migration, and enhanced peak elasticity, reducing rates by 35% and saving $45.00M annually.On logistics cost, traffic algorithms integrated Zone and same-island factors into recommendation strategies, reducing logistics costs in ID/VN by 8.41% and saving $12.52M annually.Logistics dispatch algorithms were upgraded and extended to instant and same-day scenarios, delivering $26.90M in annual savings.This cost optimization initiative aligned with SEA’s transition from scale-driven growth to high-quality growth, striking a sustainable balance between expansion and profitability while establishing a reusable methodology and cross-team collaboration model for future efficiency improvements.

H2项目奖.reason: Starting in 2025, the team launched the 0–1 build of intelligent review, leveraging AI to reconstruct the entire review workflow and process. This drove comprehensive improvements in manual review cost, efficiency, turnaround time, and overall accuracy.Capability Upgrade The moderation process evolved from a traditional ML-driven 1.0 stage to an LLM-led 2.0 stage. The team built an LLM Studio tailored for TTS e-commerce review, established supporting operational processes and a self-developed foundational model, and rapidly incubated an in-house PE team capable of LLM operations to enable scaled deployment.Intelligent moderation expanded from single-text, English-only, and access-control scenarios to multimodal content (text + image + video clips) and all-scene moderation processes (recall + specialist review). Through master–sub prompts and LLM multilingual capabilities, the system now supports full-language coverage across US, SEA, EMEA, LATAM, and JP.Results AchievedFrom July to December, intelligent moderation scaled aggressively and delivered significant gains:Global enqueue rate reduced by 51% (1.2% → 0.59%)LLM automated decision rate increased from 11.1% to 74.3% (+63.2pp)BPO HC reduced by 1,258 (4,122 → 2,864)HC per million orders reduced by 46% (3.9 → 2.1)Accuracy maintained at high levels: Through proactive sampling QA and feedback loops via ecosystem metrics (WLR / EROR / ASR), the model was continuously optimized, with PE accuracy consistently above 93%.Turnaround time reduced: Overall moderation time decreased by 32 minutes (LLM: 23 minutes vs. global average manual review: 2.42 hours).Total cost significantly reduced: By strengthening PE capability through training, optimizing token usage, and improving in-house model performance in collaboration with the algorithm team, overall moderation cost decreased by 47.1% (0.342% → 0.181%).Human cost rate decreased by 63.4% (0.153% → 0.056%)Machine cost rate decreased by 33.7% (0.190% → 0.126%)PE cost per task reduced by 83.8% ($0.21 → $0.034), compared to human cost per task rising from $0.24 → $0.28Human/PE ROI improved from 1.14 to 8.14.

The team built an LLM Studio tailored for TTS e-commerce review, established supporting operational processes and a self-developed foundational model, and rapidly incubated an in-house PE team capable of LLM operations to enable scaled deployment.Intelligent moderation expanded from single-text, English-only, and access-control scenarios to multimodal content (text + image + video clips) and all-scene moderation processes (recall + specialist review). Through master–sub prompts and LLM multilingual capabilities, the system now supports full-language coverage across US, SEA, EMEA, LATAM, and JP.Results AchievedFrom July to December, intelligent moderation scaled aggressively and delivered significant gains:Global enqueue rate reduced by 51% (1.2% → 0.59%)LLM automated decision rate increased from 11.1% to 74.3% (+63.2pp)BPO HC reduced by 1,258 (4,122 → 2,864)HC per million orders reduced by 46% (3.9 → 2.1)Accuracy maintained at high levels: Through proactive sampling QA and feedback loops via ecosystem metrics (WLR / EROR / ASR), the model was continuously optimized, with PE accuracy consistently above 93%.Turnaround time reduced: Overall moderation time decreased by 32 minutes (LLM: 23 minutes vs. global average manual review: 2.42 hours).Total cost significantly reduced: By strengthening PE capability through training, optimizing token usage, and improving in-house model performance in collaboration with the algorithm team, overall moderation cost decreased by 47.1% (0.342% → 0.181%).Human cost rate decreased by 63.4% (0.153% → 0.056%)Machine cost rate decreased by 33.7% (0.190% → 0.126%)PE cost per task reduced by 83.8% ($0.21 → $0.034), compared to human cost per task rising from $0.24 → $0.28Human/PE ROI improved from 1.14 to 8.14.

Through master–sub prompts and LLM multilingual capabilities, the system now supports full-language coverage across US, SEA, EMEA, LATAM, and JP.Results AchievedFrom July to December, intelligent moderation scaled aggressively and delivered significant gains:Global enqueue rate reduced by 51% (1.2% → 0.59%)LLM automated decision rate increased from 11.1% to 74.3% (+63.2pp)BPO HC reduced by 1,258 (4,122 → 2,864)HC per million orders reduced by 46% (3.9 → 2.1)Accuracy maintained at high levels: Through proactive sampling QA and feedback loops via ecosystem metrics (WLR / EROR / ASR), the model was continuously optimized, with PE accuracy consistently above 93%.Turnaround time reduced: Overall moderation time decreased by 32 minutes (LLM: 23 minutes vs. global average manual review: 2.42 hours).Total cost significantly reduced: By strengthening PE capability through training, optimizing token usage, and improving in-house model performance in collaboration with the algorithm team, overall moderation cost decreased by 47.1% (0.342% → 0.181%).Human cost rate decreased by 63.4% (0.153% → 0.056%)Machine cost rate decreased by 33.7% (0.190% → 0.126%)PE cost per task reduced by 83.8% ($0.21 → $0.034), compared to human cost per task rising from $0.24 → $0.28Human/PE ROI improved from 1.14 to 8.14.
